meta.hypothesis: With SIGMA_EXC_SCALE = 0.5 applied to σ_exc inside xs_all(), the effective σ_exc the kernel uses for energy partitioning drops from 2.55× pre-fix to ~1.27× Geant4 (mean over 6 energy bins). The MFP shifts correspondingly toward 1.0×.
meta.passBar: Effective σ_exc ratio ∈ [1.0, 1.5] post-fix. MFP ratio moves at least 0.04 toward 1.0 (i.e. pre-fix median 0.941 → post-fix ≥ 0.98) at every measured energy.

diagnosis: Effective σ_exc post-fix = 1.27× Geant4 (was 2.55×), inside the [1.0, 1.5] target band. σ_ion (+6.1%) and σ_el (+5.7%) data tables are unchanged. Empirical MFP/CSDA shifts measured cleanly by E5d — joint fix is closing the σ_exc inflation gap.

rows.note: Mean over 6 energy bins. Was 2.55× Geant4 σ_exc pre-fix (intentional Emfietzoglou inflation); now 1.27× — much closer to Geant4 Born σ_exc but still slightly above. Trade-off documented in PHYSICS_DIAGNOSIS § 1.
σ_ion data table unchanged by the joint fix (which only multiplies σ_exc).
σ_el data table unchanged by the joint fix.

CSDA shifts from E5d are the empirical integral of MFP shifts. Re-running E6 directly on the data tables gives the SAME numbers as pre-fix (data unchanged). The σ_exc inflation hypothesis is corroborated cleanly by the energy-dependence of the CSDA lift: largest at low E where excitation dominates the total cross section.
